Section 3 A lecturer is a faculty member who has earned units and
is currently pursuing MA/MS degree program
aligned/allied to his/her field of specialization. A senior
lecturer is a full-fledged MA/MS degree holder
aligned/allied to his/her field of specialization whose performance and accomplishments are comparable to at least an Assistant Professor I rank. A professional lecturer
is full-fledged PhD/EdD or other doctorate degree holder
whose performance and accomplishments.
V. General Policies
Section 1 All faculty members holding permanent positions/ items
must first be given regular WL for the semester before
additional teachers (under Contract of Service) will be
hired.
Section 2 Faculty members must be informed of their tentative
teaching loads (TL) at least one (1) week before the start of classes.
Section 3 The percentage distribution of job function vis- à-vis major
thrusts of the University shall be specified in the Individual
Performance Commitment and Review (IPCR).
Section 4 A faculty member with a WL & TL in excess of 30

hours/week within office hours, after office hours on a scheduled workday, on a rest day or scheduled day off, holiday, or special non-working day shall be entitled to an honorarium/ overtime pay for the teaching overload subject to the availability of funds or is granted service credit equivalence.
Section 5 All other assignments with monetary remuneration shall
not be given any workload equivalent.
Section 6 The IGP Manual of the University shall be used as guide
when a faculty member is involved in Production function.
Section 7 A faculty member must satisfy the mandatory functions
before accepting optional loads.
Section 8 In the exigency of the service, additional loads may be
given to faculty with more than the minimum WL.

fixed_blocks.text:
5 a rank of “Lecturer”, “Senior Lecturer”, or “Professorial Lecturer” (UP Faculty Manual).

Section 3 A lecturer is a faculty member who has earned units and is currently pursuing MA/MS degree program aligned/allied to his/her field of specialization. A senior lecturer is a full-fledged MA/MS degree holder aligned/allied to his/her field of specialization whose performance and accomplishments are comparable to at least an Assistant Professor I rank. A professional lecturer is a full-fledged PhD/EdD or other doctorate degree holder whose performance and accomplishments.

V. General Policies

Section 1 All faculty members holding permanent positions/items must first be given regular WL for the semester before additional teachers (under Contract of Service) will be hired. Section 2 Faculty members must be informed of their tentative teaching loads (TL) at least one (1) week before the start of classes. Section 3 The percentage distribution of job function vis-à-vis major thrusts of the University shall be specified in the Individual Performance Commitment and Review (IPCR). Section 4 A faculty member with a WL & TL in excess of 30 hours/week within office hours, after office hours on a scheduled workday, on a rest day or scheduled day off, holiday, or special non-working day shall be entitled to an honorarium/overtime pay for the teaching overload subject to the availability of funds or is granted service credit equivalence. Section 5 All other assignments with monetary remuneration shall not be given any workload equivalent. Section 6 The IGP Manual of the University shall be used as guide when a faculty member is involved in Production function. Section 7 A faculty member must satisfy the mandatory functions before accepting optional loads. Section 8 In the exigency of the service, additional loads may be given to faculty with more than the minimum WL.

qa_pairs.context:
5 a rank of “Lecturer”, “Senior Lecturer”, or “Professorial Lecturer” (UP Faculty Manual).

Section 3 A lecturer is a faculty member who has earned units and is currently pursuing MA/MS degree program aligned/allied to his/her field of specialization. A senior lecturer is a full-fledged MA/MS degree holder aligned/allied to his/her field of specialization whose performance and accomplishments are comparable to at least an Assistant Professor I rank. A professional lecturer is a full-fledged PhD/EdD or other doctorate degree holder whose performance and accomplishments.

V. General Policies

Section 1 All faculty members holding permanent positions/items must first be given regular WL for the semester before additional teachers (under Contract of Service) will be hired. Section 2 Faculty members must be informed of their tentative teaching loads (TL) at least one (1) week before the start of classes. Section 3 The percentage distribution of job function vis-à-vis major thrusts of the University shall be specified in the Individual Performance Commitment and Review (IPCR). Section 4 A faculty member with a WL & TL in excess of 30 hours/week within office hours, after office hours on a scheduled workday, on a rest day or scheduled day off, holiday, or special non-working day shall be entitled to an honorarium/overtime pay for the teaching overload subject to the availability of funds or is granted service credit equivalence. Section 5 All other assignments with monetary remuneration shall not be given any workload equivalent. Section 6 The IGP Manual of the University shall be used as guide when a faculty member is involved in Production function. Section 7 A faculty member must satisfy the mandatory functions before accepting optional loads. Section 8 In the exigency of the service, additional loads may be given to faculty with more than the minimum WL.
qa_pairs.question: What is the minimum WL a faculty member must have to be eligible for additional loads?
qa_pairs.answer: The minimum WL for a faculty member to be eligible for additional loads is 30 hours/week within office hours, after office hours on a scheduled workday, on a rest day or scheduled day off, holiday, or special non-working day.

Section 8 In the exigency of the service, additional loads may be given to faculty with more than the minimum WL.
qa_pairs.question: What is the criteria for determining a faculty member's rank?
qa_pairs.answer: A faculty member's rank is determined by their units earned and degree program aligned/allied to their field of specialization. A senior lecturer is a full-fledged MA/MS degree holder aligned/allied to their field of specialization whose performance and accomplishments are comparable to at least an Assistant Professor I rank.

qa_pairs.question: What is the purpose of the Individual Performance Commitment and Review (IPCR)?
qa_pairs.answer: The IPCR manual of the University shall be used as a guide when a faculty member is involved in Production function. The IPCR is used to specify the percentage distribution of job functions vis-à-vis major thrusts of the University.

qa_pairs.question: How many hours/week within office hours, after office hours on a scheduled workday, on a rest day or scheduled day off, holiday, or special non-working day is considered a normal teaching load?
qa_pairs.answer: A faculty member's normal teaching load is 30 hours/week within office hours, after office hours on a scheduled workday, on a rest day or scheduled day off, holiday, or special non-working day.

qa_pairs.question: How many rest days or scheduled day off are considered normal?
qa_pairs.answer: A faculty member's normal rest days or scheduled day off are two (2) days per week.

qa_pairs.question: What is the policy on service credit equivalence?
qa_pairs.answer: Service credit equivalence is granted to a faculty member who has rendered at least three (3) years of continuous and satisfactory service in their current rank.

qa_pairs.question: How many hours/week within office hours, after office hours on a scheduled workday, on a rest day or scheduled day off, holiday, or special non-working day is considered a maximum teaching load?

qa_pairs.question: How many units are required to earn a rank of Lecturer?

qa_pairs.answer: The IGP Manual of the University shall be used as guide when a faculty member is involved in Production function.

5 a rank of “Lecturer”, “Senior Lecturer”, or “Professorial Lecturer” (UP Faculty Manual).

Section 3 A lecturer is a faculty member who has earned units and is currently pursuing MA/MS degree program aligned/allied to his/her field of specialization. A senior lecturer is a full-fledged MA/MS degree holder aligned/allied to his/her field of specialization whose performance and accomplishments are comparable to at least an Assistant Professor I rank. A professional lecturer is a full-fledged PhD/EdD or other doctorate degree holder whose performance and accomplishments.

V. General Policies

Section 1 All faculty members holding permanent positions/items must first be given regular WL for the semester before additional teachers (under Contract of Service) will be hired. Section 2 Faculty members must be informed of their tentative teaching loads (TL) at least one (1) week before the start of classes. Section 3 The percentage distribution of job function vis-à-vis major thrusts of the University shall be specified in the Individual Performance Commitment and Review (IPCR). Section 4 A faculty member with a WL & TL in excess of 30 hours/week within office hours, after office hours on a scheduled workday, on a rest day or scheduled day off, holiday, or special non-working day shall be entitled to an honorarium/overtime pay for the teaching overload subject to the availability of funds or is granted service credit equivalence. Section 5 All other assignments with monetary remuneration shall not be given any workload equivalent. Section 6 The IGP Manual of the University shall be used as guide when a faculty member is involved in Production function. Section 7 A faculty member must satisfy the mandatory functions before accepting optional loads. Section 8 In the exigency of the service, additional loads may be given to faculty with more than the minimum WL.
qa_pairs.question: How many hours/week within office hours, after office hours on a scheduled workday, on a rest day or scheduled day off, holiday, or special non-working day is considered a minimum teaching load?
qa_pairs.answer: A faculty member's minimum teaching load is 20 hours/week within office hours, after office hours on a scheduled workday, on a rest day or scheduled day off, holiday, or special non-working day.

qa_pairs.question: What is the policy on service credit equivalence for additional loads?
qa_pairs.answer: Service credit equivalence is granted to a faculty member who has rendered at least three (3) years of continuous and satisfactory service in their current rank.

qa_pairs.question: How many rest days or scheduled day off are considered normal for a faculty member with a WL & TL in excess of 30 hours/week within office hours, after office hours on a scheduled workday, on a rest day or scheduled day off, holiday, or special non-working day?
qa_pairs.answer: A faculty member's normal rest days or scheduled day off are two (2) days per week.
